# Pelagora

> Pelagora is an open source decentralized commerce network. Every node is a marketplace.

## What is Pelagora?

Pelagora is an open protocol and network for decentralized commerce. Anyone can run a Beacon (a self-hosted node) to publish and discover items across the network. AI agents can participate natively via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## How It Works

- **Beacons**: Self-hosted nodes that publish items to the network
- **PIM Protocol**: A shared product information schema built on Schema.org standards
- **MCP Server**: Allows AI agents (Claude, GPT, etc.) to search, browse, and transact on the network
- **AI Skill**: A Claude Code skill for interacting with the Pelagora network

## Content Available

- `/blog` — Articles about decentralized commerce, building on Pelagora, and network updates
- `/build` — Guide for developers to build on the Pelagora network
- `/contribute` — How to contribute to the open source project
- `/ai-skill` — Information about the Pelagora AI Skill for Claude
- `/buy-or-sell` — How to buy and sell on the network
- `/sitemap.xml` — Full sitemap of all pages and blog posts

## For AI Agents

Pelagora is designed for AI agent participation. The MCP server provides programmatic access to:

- Search listings across the network
- View item details with full structured data
- Discover Beacons and their inventories

### Resources

- MCP Server: https://github.com/ReffoAI/pelagora-mcp-server
- PIM Protocol: https://github.com/ReffoAI/pim-protocol
- CLI Installer: https://github.com/ReffoAI/pelagora-cli-installer
- Documentation: https://docs.pelagora.org
